channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Inside St. John the Baptist Chapel Built by the Kenyatta Family in Ichaweri
item.description: Nestled in the peaceful green hills of Ichaweri village, Gatundu South, Kiambu County, St. John the Baptist Chapel is a special family tribute to Kenya’s founding father, Mzee Jomo Kenyatta. The chape...

item.title: Cardiologists urge patients to cook at home and reduce ultra-processed food consumption
item.description: A new clinical consensus statement from the European Society of Cardiology and the European Association of Preventive Cardiology highlights the health risks of ultra-processed foods (UPFs) and offers ...

item.title: Canadians Unite to Support Montreal Canadiens in Stanley Cup Playoffs
item.description: Across Canada, hockey fans are rallying behind the Montreal Canadiens as they remain the only Canadian team contending for the NHL’s Stanley Cup this season. Fans from Edmonton, Calgary, Winnipeg, and...

item.title: Former British police officer hospitalised following cruise ship rat virus evacuation
item.description: The news report concerns a former British police officer who is currently being kept in isolation in a hospital following an evacuation from a cruise ship affected by a rat-borne virus. While details ...

item.title: Amazon Begins Limited Drone Delivery Service in Darlington
item.description: Amazon has launched its first drone delivery service in the UK, starting with a trial in Darlington, County Durham. The service delivers parcels under 5lb (2.2kg), including everyday items such as bat...

item.title: Ogun Deputy Governor Salako-Oyedele Declares Interest in Ogun West Senate Seat
item.description: Political matter for Ogun West don heat up as the Deputy Governor of Ogun State, Engr. Noimot Salako-Oyedele, don officially join the race for the senatorial seat ahead of the 2027 elections. Reports ...

item.title: Five Individuals, Including Former Police Officers, Sentenced for Interfering in 2018 Murder Investigation
item.description: In a significant ruling, five people, including two former police officers, have been sentenced to 17 years’ imprisonment for interfering in the investigation of the 2018 murder of KwaDengezi communit...

item.title: Second Day of Breathe Pakistan Climate Conference Focuses on Global South Cooperation and Policy Dialogue
item.description: The second day of the Breathe Pakistan International Climate Change Conference, organised by DawnMedia, is underway in Islamabad, bringing together a wide range of stakeholders to address Pakistan’s g...

item.title: Australia Men’s Cricket Team to Play Three ODIs in Pakistan from May 30
item.description: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has officially confirmed that the Australian men's cricket team will tour Pakistan for a three-match One Day International (ODI) series. The visiting team is set to ar...

item.title: Pakistan Faces Disproportionate Climate Crisis Amid Global Inaction
item.description: Pakistan is facing a severe climate crisis that is not isolated but rather interconnected, with multiple dimensions impacting the country simultaneously. Despite contributing less than 1 percent to gl...

item.title: Authorities Release Possible Suicide Note from Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Access to the full Sky News article is currently blocked, but based on the available information, the report concerns the potential release of a suicide note linked to Jeffrey Epstein, the financier a...

item.title: ISPR Highlights Pakistan’s Preparedness and Resolve on First Anniversary of Marka-i-Haq
item.description: On the first anniversary of the 2025 military conflict with India, known as Marka-i-Haq, Pakistan’s Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) reaffirmed the country’s readiness to counter any hostile act...

item.title: Kenyan Farmer Alleges Harassment and Loss of Documents After Encounters with Police in Gambia
item.description: A Kenyan farmer living and working in The Gambia has come out to narrate a troubling experience with local police that left him feeling unsafe and at risk of deportation. The man, identified as Ken, s...

item.title: Daily Horoscope Guidance for May 7, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail presents daily horoscopes for May 7, 2026, offering guidance based on astrological influences. Individuals with birthdays today are encouraged to channel their passion positively, b...

item.title: PM Shehbaz's daughter and son-in-law cleared by anti-corruption court
item.description: In Lahore today, the Anti-Corruption Court (ATC) formally acquitted Rabia Imran, daughter of Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, and her husband Ali Imran Yousaf from alleged irregularities linked to the P...

item.title: SSANU and NASU Suspend Strike to Allow Government Negotiations
item.description: The Joint Action Committee (JAC) of the Senior Staff Association of Nigerian Universities (SSANU) and the Non-Academic Staff Union of Educational and Associated Institutions (NASU) don announce say de...

item.title: Commission hears about mishandling of R200m cocaine by police
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry has been informed that cocaine valued at around R200 million, seized by law enforcement in 2021, was mishandled and later stolen. The drugs, initially seized at the ...

item.title: Building Pakistan’s Climate Resilience Through Education and STEM
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan’s urgent need to build climate resilience by learning from countries like Iran, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Vietnam. It emphasises that true resilience is not only about ...

item.title: Trump's China Visit Will Be Shaped by the Iran Conflict
item.description: The article explains that the war involving Iran is expected to be a major issue during President Trump’s upcoming visit to China. According to the report, recent signs of progress toward restarting p...

item.title: Italian and Canadian Officials Visit Saskatchewan to Strengthen Ties
item.description: Representatives from Italy and Canada recently visited several cities in Saskatchewan to strengthen bilateral relations and explore potential collaboration in economic, cultural, and social sectors. T...
